hypothesis: If universal machine learning interatomic potentials are evaluated on metal-organic frameworks, then models trained on larger and more chemically diverse datasets (particularly those incorporating OMat24 or Alexandria data) with equivariant graph neural network architectures will demonstrate superior accuracy in structural optimization, mechanical properties, and thermodynamic predictions compared to models trained solely on MPtrj, because MOFs contain complex metal coordination environments and organic linkers that require broader training distribution coverage to accurately capture the relevant potential energy surfaces.
